name: CogAttentionBench
description: CogAttentionBench is a benchmark for probing cognitive attention mechanisms in frontier AI models. It comprises 38 items across five tasks grounded in established experimental psychology paradigms: selective attention (Stroop/flanker interference), attention shifting (task-switching), sustained attention (continuous performance), inattentional blindness (gorilla paradigm), and saliency awareness (feature integration). Unlike needle-in-a-haystack tests that rely on explicit markers, CogAttentionBench creates genuine interference by exploiting LLM-specific processing biases (statistical frequency traps, context priming, expectation violation) that function as analogs to the automatic processing underlying human interference effects. A hardened variant adds 90 items across five difficulty tiers for diagnostic stratification of frontier models.
citeAs: @inproceedings{zeng2026cogattentionbench, title={CogAttentionBench: Probing Cognitive Attention Mechanisms in Frontier AI Models}, author={Zeng, Zihan}, booktitle={Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), Datasets and Benchmarks Track}, year={2026}, url={https://github.com/Lumen-Nox/CogAttentionBench}}

distribution.name: github-repository
distribution.description: Source repository hosted on GitHub containing all benchmark items, evaluation notebooks, and scoring code.

distribution.name: selective-attention-json
distribution.description: JSON file containing the selective attention task items (Stroop-like interference, statistical frequency traps, emotional salience).

distribution.name: sustained-attention-json
distribution.description: JSON file containing the sustained attention (CPT-style) items, including needle-in-haystack arithmetic and factual retrieval embedded in 0-7 paragraphs of filler with decoy numbers.

distribution.name: task-notebooks
distribution.description: Jupyter notebooks (Kaggle Benchmarks SDK / kbench compatible) implementing each of the five attention tasks.

recordSet.name: selective-attention-items
recordSet.description: 14 items testing selective attention. Each item provides interfering text, a question, the expected answer, the distractor mechanism, and difficulty.

field.name: text
field.description: Stimulus text containing the target embedded among interfering distractors.

field.name: question
field.description: Task instruction posed to the model.

field.name: answer
field.description: Ground-truth answer used for exact-match scoring.

field.name: distractor_type
field.description: Category of interference: stroop_color_word, emotional_salience, statistical_frequency_trap, context_priming, anchoring, negation, etc.

field.name: difficulty
field.description: Difficulty tier (medium / hard / very_hard / extreme / ultimate).

recordSet.name: sustained-attention-items
recordSet.description: 8 sustained attention items embedding an arithmetic or factual needle in 0-7 paragraphs of filler text with decoy numerical distractors.

field.name: text
field.description: Composite prompt containing filler paragraphs and the embedded needle.

field.name: answer
field.description: Ground-truth answer (substring matching with formatting normalization).

field.name: n_filler
field.description: Number of filler paragraphs surrounding the needle (0-7).

rai:dataCollection: Items were hand-crafted by the author to instantiate established experimental-psychology paradigms (Stroop 1935; Eriksen & Eriksen 1974; Rogers & Monsell 1995; Rosvold et al. 1956; Simons & Chabris 1999; Treisman & Gelade 1980). Sustained-attention items use seeded procedural generation (seed=42) for filler-paragraph assembly while keeping decoy properties controlled.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Each item has a single unambiguous ground-truth answer. Items were manually verified to (a) contain the intended interference type and (b) have a single correct answer that requires overriding the interference.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: No preprocessing of model outputs beyond whitespace normalization and case folding for substring matching. Scoring is exact-match (selective, inattentional blindness primary task), substring (sustained), proportional (shifting), or weighted multi-component (saliency).
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Maintained by the author at https://github.com/Lumen-Nox/CogAttentionBench. Versioned releases are mirrored to Zenodo with persistent DOIs.
rai:dataLimitations: (1) English-only stimuli. (2) Static item set may be subject to future training-data contamination. (3) 38 items is small by psychometric standards (hardened v2 adds 90 items). (4) Single-trial deterministic evaluation (temperature 0) does not capture model output variance. (5) Functional equivalence to human attention is behavioral, not mechanistic.
rai:dataSocialImpact: The benchmark is intended to inform AI-safety-relevant deployment decisions by exposing dissociable attention failure modes in frontier models. It uses no human subjects, no personal data, and contains no offensive or dual-use content.
rai:dataUseCases: Evaluating cognitive attention in LLMs; diagnostic stratification of frontier models; testing predictions of the Magnetic Field of Attention (MFA) framework; comparison of LLM attention profiles to human neuropsychological dissociation patterns.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. The benchmark uses fictional entities where possible to prevent contamination via training-data knowledge.
